title: Savory Slow Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Moroccan dish made with lamb, sweet rice flour chapssalgaru, and Mediterranean spices, slow cooked in a single pot to create a rich and savory stew.
instructions: Heat the oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the diced lardons and cook until they are crispy and golden brown.
Remove the lardons from the pot and set them aside on a paper towel lined plate.
Add the lamb to the pot and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper and cook for 1 minute.
Add the sweet rice flour to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the cooked lardons and serve the tagine hot over couscous or rice.
